title: W1 GDD artifact (slim gameDNA + milestones + runtime assertions)
description: The single design artifact written by the W1 Spec node (spec/gdd.json). A slim, template-constrained subset of gameDNA plus the milestone plan that is the spine of W2/W4/W5. Authored by packages/skills/write-gdd/SKILL.md. Every field has exactly one downstream consumer.

meta.description: Game identity. archetype/coreLoop/coreVerb are carried verbatim from classification.json; title is derived by W1.

title.description: Short game title W1 derives from the prompt (<=6 words).

archetype.description: Copied from classification.json. Routes W2 to templates/modules/<archetype>; constrains every mechanic/entity to that template's capabilities.

coreLoop.description: Copied from classification.json: the one-line loop W1 elaborates into milestones.

coreVerb.description: Copied from classification.json: the single central player verb (drives M1).

coreFantasy.description: Optional emotional hook (from classification.json if present, else omit).

failModel.description: The ONE fail-model the design chose. The HUD surfaces only the resource this drives (health⇒HP bar, lives⇒life counter, respawn⇒spawn-return/none, none⇒sandbox). Must agree with loseCondition.observable. Prevents an inert maxHealth from being rendered as a phantom health bar (frog1).

scoringModel.description: Carried from classification.json (SKILL §3.6). Decides whether/how to score. none ⇒ declare NO score (the win is the readout, no maxScore). Non-none ⇒ declare maxScore and make every reward idempotent (one-shot, respawn-safe). Never bolt a bare open counter onto a completion game.

maxScore.description: REQUIRED when scoringModel != none: the finite, reachable score ceiling = Σ of all idempotent reward values (e.g. 3 coins × 1 = 3). score is monotone 0..maxScore and never exceeds it; prefer an X/maxScore readout and tie it to the win. OMIT when scoringModel == none (no score exists). VERIFY-1 proves maxScore == Σ(reward values); VERIFY-2 asserts score ≤ maxScore.

artStyle.description: Short art-direction note (e.g. 'pixel art, bright, side-view'). Feeds W3 asset generation; 'placeholder' is valid for v1.

physicsProfile.description: Copied from classification.json. W2 sets engine/gameConfig params from this.

subMode.description: Optional within-archetype variant hint for W4 (e.g. grid_logic: 'puzzle'|'tactics'|'match'|'arcade'|'roguelike'; top_down: 'tilemap'|'arena'). Advisory in v1; omit if the base archetype suffices.

entities.description: Every game object the milestones reference. The player MUST be entity[0] (role:'player'). Each entity maps to a code file/behavior in W4 and (if it needs art) an asset slot in assetList.

id.description: Stable identifier used by assertions and assetList (e.g. 'player', 'coin', 'spike', 'enemy_walker'). snake_case.

role.description: Functional role. Constrains which template behavior W4 composes onto it.

description.description: One line: what it is and what it does in the loop.

behaviors.description: Template behaviors/capabilities to compose (e.g. ['PlatformerMovement','MeleeAttack'] or ['PatrolAI']). MUST name capabilities the chosen template provides — never invent. Omit for inert entities.

assetSlot.description: Key into assetList for this entity's sprite, if it needs art. Omit for code-drawn/placeholder entities.

mechanics.description: The named systems the game needs, each constrained to a template capability. Slim CustomMechanic shape (ForgeDNA). One mechanic per real interaction; respect the scope-cut (no mechanic the prompt+loop don't demand).

description.description: Plain-language rule: what triggers it and what observable effect it has (the rule W4 implements and W5 may assert).

capability.description: The template behavior/hook this maps to (e.g. 'PlatformerMovement.jump', 'onEntityEnteredCell'). Optional until capabilities.md exists; when present, must exist in the template.

controls.description: Input scheme (desktop keyboard/mouse only for v1). Each row is what an input does — and is the menu of inputs assertions may fire.

input.description: Key or button (e.g. 'ArrowLeft','ArrowRight','Space','ArrowUp','MouseClick'). Use DOM/Phaser key names so W5 can fire them.

action.description: What the input does (e.g. 'move left','jump','place selected tower').

winCondition.description: How the player wins. 'observable' is the live-game-object signal that becomes the final milestone's win assertion.

description.description: Plain language (e.g. 'reach the exit door', 'clear all boxes onto targets', 'survive all waves').

observable.description: The checkable signal on the game object, e.g. "__GAME__.status === 'won'" or "__GAME__.score >= 100".

loseCondition.description: How the player loses / the loop resets. 'observable' becomes the final milestone's lose assertion. If the game truly cannot be lost, set description to 'none' and observable to the reset signal instead.

config.description: Tuning numbers W2 merges into the template gameConfig.json (flat key->number; W2 wraps in {value:X}). Only game-specific values; never screenSize/debugConfig/renderConfig. Keys must match the archetype's config schema (e.g. platformer: gravityY,jumpPower,walkSpeed; grid_logic: cellSize,maxMoves; tower_defense: startingGold,startingLives).

assetList.description: The art/audio the game needs, as slots. Becomes W2's index.json slots and W3's generation list. Empty array is valid (W3 fills placeholders).

type.description: Asset kind. Drives W3 generation params and W2 index.json dims.

description.description: Generation prompt: what it depicts + view direction (e.g. 'side-view hero facing right', 'top-down coin', 'tileable grass background'). Archetype-conditioned (platformer=side-view; top_down/grid/TD=top-down; ui_heavy=front bust).

frames.description: For type:'animation' — frame names (e.g. ['idle','run','jump']). Omit otherwise.

width.description: Optional pixel width hint for W2 index.json.

milestones.description: The spine: 3-5 PLAYABLE vertical slices (default 3, never fewer than 3 — every game exercises the reused verify harness across ≥3 stages), in build order. INVARIANTS: milestones[0] (M1) = the core loop playable ('it plays at all'); the LAST milestone includes an end-state (win and/or lose). Each is a runnable checkpoint with acceptance criteria + executable runtime assertions. Sized to the post-scope-cut design; overflow goes back to scope-cut, the list never exceeds 5.

id.description: Milestone id 'M1','M2',... in build order. M1 is the core loop.

name.description: Short outcome name (e.g. 'Move and jump', 'Collect + hazards', 'Win/lose + restart').

goal.description: The PLAYABLE outcome: 'at the end of this, the game runs and the player can <do X>'. An outcome, not a task.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The definition of done for this slice, as human-readable checkable bullets (1:1 with assertions). Each must be observable behavior, never an implementation detail.

assertions.description: Executable runtime checks W5 runs headless against window.__GAME__ after firing input. 1:1 with acceptanceCriteria. Each: setup -> input -> observe -> expect (the Given/When/Then of a game test).

describe.description: Human-readable predicate = the failure message (e.g. 'pressing Up decreases player.y within 300ms').

setup.description: GIVEN — optional precondition before input. Omit if the fresh scene already satisfies it.

scene.description: Scene/level to boot if not the default.

state.description: Field->value mutations to apply on __GAME__ before the input (e.g. {"player.health": 1}). Use sparingly; prefer natural state.

input.description: WHEN — the synthetic input/event to fire. Omit for at-scene-start checks (then this is a pure observe).

key.description: For keyPress/keyHold: DOM/Phaser key name (e.g. 'ArrowUp','Space'). Must appear in controls[].

durationMs.description: For keyHold: how long to hold before observing (e.g. 300).

target.description: For click/event: the entity id or event name (e.g. 'coin', 'overlap:player,coin').

observe.description: THEN — the observable on the live game object to read (e.g. 'player.y', 'score', 'entities.count(type==enemy)', 'status'). Reads __GAME__ accessors only, never engine internals.

expect.description: The expected change/value of `observe`. Exactly one comparator key set.

decreases.description: observe value is lower after input than before.

increases.description: observe value is higher after input than before.

unchanged.description: observe value is the same after input.
